scope_note: QA Orbit Grade Decomposition cert [298]. Primary sources: Hardy, G.H. and Wright, E.M. (2008) An Introduction to the Theory of Numbers, Oxford, ISBN 978-0-19-921986-5 Ch.IV (p-adic valuation, gcd invariants); Wall, D.D. (1960) Fibonacci Primitive Roots, Amer. Math. Monthly 67(6):525-532 DOI 10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541 (Pisano period pi(9)=24, orbit periods). CLAIM (narrow, falsifiable): The three QA orbits on {1,...,9}^2 are exactly stratified by v3(gcd(b,e)) -- the 3-adic valuation of gcd(b,e): (C1) v3(gcd) is preserved by T (QA T-step), verified exhaustively for all 81 states; algebraic proof: gcd(e, b+e)=gcd(e,b)=gcd(b,e). (C2) Arithmetic stratification: {v3=2}=Singularity (9|b and 9|e, 1 state), {v3=1}=Satellite (3|b and 3|e, not both 9, 8 states), {v3=0}=Cosmos (3 does not divide gcd, 72 states); exhaustive partition of all 81 states. (C3) Period matches stratum: all v3=2 states period 1, all v3=1 states period 8, all v3=0 states period 24. (C4) Even/odd T-step split: within each v3=0 orbit (Cosmos, period 24), even-index states form one set of 12 and odd-index states form a disjoint set of 12; within the v3=1 orbit (Satellite, period 8), even-index=4, odd-index=4. (C5) M^12 = -I mod 9 exactly: computing M^12 as integer matrix gives diagonal entries 8 mod 9 = -1 mod 9, off-diagonal entries 0 mod 9; this is the grade-inversion / antipodal map. Mechanism: Theorem NT maintained; all computations on integer pairs. Builds on cert [291] (Fibonacci orbit periods, M order 24), cert [296] (SL(2,Z) versor isomorphism, T=M), cert [297] (Pell rotor A=M applied twice, det alternates). Lineage: derived 2026-06-02.
